zh.lifeSummary: Adela "Princess of The English, Countess of Blois, Chartres, and Meaux" of Normandy於大約1062年出生在法國諾門迪。她和Stephan DE BLOIS至少有1個兒子。她去世於1137年3月8日在法國o-阿爾培斯洛易享年76歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Adela "Princess of The English, Countess of Blois, Chartres, and Meaux" of Normandy于大约1062年出生在法国諾門迪。她和Stephan DE BLOIS至少有1个儿子。她去世于1137年3月8日在法国o-阿爾培斯洛易享年76岁。

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Normandy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Normandy in northern France. This surname is now rare in Britain.
Americanized form of French   Normandin  .

variants.name: Normandin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Alain, Serge, Adrien, Andre, Aurore, Celine, Clovis, Emile, Gaetan, Gilles, Laurier.
French: from a diminutive of   Normand  . Compare   Normandy  .

nameMeanings.name: Adela
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   Adele  , especially popular in the late 19th century.

variants.name: Adella
meanings: form of   Adele  , especially popular in the late 19th century.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

nameMeanings.name: The
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Either a variant spelling of Ty or a transferred use of the surname, a local name for someone who lived by a common pasture, Middle English tye.

nameMeanings.name: English,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Welsh: ethnic name from Middle English English, Inglish, Inglis ‘English’ (Old English Englisc), sometimes alternating with Anglo-Norman French Engleis, Engles, Anglais, Angles (Old French Englois). Compare Inglis and England . Among the aristocracy and upper gentry it marked out a man of English ancestry from one of Norman or continental origin. In counties bordering England with Scotland and Wales the name distinguished an Englishman from a Scot or a native Welshman on both sides of the border. The name may also have been acquired by English merchants who traded abroad or who lived and worked in a ‘French’ borough in England (one exclusively administered by Normans).
Irish: in Ireland, this name was used to denote an Englishman, often being adopted for Irish Aingléis ‘Englishman’ or through mistranslation for Mac an Ghallóglaigh, see Gallogly and Golightly .

variants.name: Engles
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Michel, Remi.
English and Scottish: variant of   English   or   Inglis  .
Altered form of German and Dutch   Engels  .

variants.name: Inglis
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire and Midlothian): ethnic name from Scots Inglis ‘English(man)’. Compare English .

variants.name: England
meanings: English: habitational name from Middle English Engelond ‘England’. It was probably a formal alternative to English , which is also well evidenced as a surname in England. These names may have been acquired by English landowners who moved in Norman social circles or who lived in a neighbouring country (Scotland, Wales, or Ireland), or by English merchants who traded abroad.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of various farmsteads so named, from Old Norse eng ‘meadow’ + land ‘land’.
Swedish: ornamental name with the same meaning as 2.

nameMeanings.name: Countess
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Nottinghamshire, of Norman origin): from Middle English contas(e), Old French contesse ‘countess’, applied as a nickname for a proud, haughty woman or for a foppish man, or as an occupational name for a servant of a countess.

variants.name: Countiss
meanings: English: variant of   Countess  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

nameMeanings.name: Blois,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: = Latin Aloysius, probably from Germanic Alwisi ‘all wise’.

nameMeanings.name: Chartres,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: French, English: from the French place-name Chartres. See Charters .

variants.name: Charters
meanings: Scottish (Kirkcudbrightshire) and northern English: habitational name of Norman origin from the French city of Chartres, named for a Gaulish tribe, recorded in Latin sources as the Carnutes, whose seat it was.
English: variant of   Chatters  .

nameMeanings.name: and
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Hebrew. See Anne .

nameMeanings.name: Meaux"
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andrus, Amede, Camille, Eves, Maudry, Michel, Pierre, Theogene.
French:
variant of Maux, probably a nickname from a variant of mal ‘bad’ (see Mau ), or a habitational name from a place called Maux or perhaps Meaux (see 2 below).

variants.name: Mau
meanings: Pet form of Maria and Margit , now also used as an independent given name and as an element of compound names such as Mai-Britt, Mai-Lis, Anne-Mai, and Britt-Mai.

en.lifeSummary: Adela "Princess of The English, Countess of Blois, Chartres, and Meaux" of Normandy was born about 1062, in Normandy, France. She had at least 1 son with Stephan DE BLOIS. She died on 8 March 1137, in Loire, Rhône-Alpes, France, at the age of 76.

fr.lifeSummary: Adela "Princess of The English, Countess of Blois, Chartres, and Meaux" of Normandy est née environ 1062, à Normandie, France. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Stephan DE BLOIS. Elle est décédée le 8 mars 1137, à Loire, Rhône-Alpes, France, à l’âge de 76 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Adela "Princess of The English, Countess of Blois, Chartres, and Meaux" of Normandy wurde um 1062 in Normandie, Frankreich geboren. Sie hatte mit Stephan DE BLOIS mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb am 8. März 1137 im Alter von 76 Jahren in Loire, Rhône-Alpes, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Adela "Princess of The English, Countess of Blois, Chartres, and Meaux" of Normandy è nata il circa 1062 nel Normandie, Francia. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Stephan DE BLOIS. Morì il 8 marzo 1137, nel Loire, Rhône-Alpes, Francia, all'età di 76 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Adela "Princess of The English, Countess of Blois, Chartres, and Meaux" of Normandyは約1062年にフランスノーマンディで生まれた。彼女はStephan DE BLOISとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1137年3月8日にフランスルhoネ-アルペスロイアーで76歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Adela "Princess of The English, Countess of Blois, Chartres, and Meaux" of Normandy(은)는 대략 1062년 프랑스 노먼디에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Stephan DE BLOIS(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1137년 3월 8일 프랑스 ho니-알프스 로이르에서 76세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Adela "Princess of The English, Countess of Blois, Chartres, and Meaux" of Normandy nasceu aproximadamente 1062, em Normandie, França. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com Stephan DE BLOIS. Ela faleceu em 8 de março de 1137, em Loire, Rhône-Alpes, França, com 76 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Adela "Princess of The English, Countess of Blois, Chartres, and Meaux" of Normandy родилась около 1062 г. в местности норманди, Франция. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Stephan DE BLOIS. Она умерла 8 марта 1137 г. в местности лоир, рoне-алпесь, Франция в возрасте 76 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Adela "Princess of The English, Countess of Blois, Chartres, and Meaux" of Normandy nació aproximadamente 1062, en Normandie, Francia. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Stephan DE BLOIS. Murió el 8 de marzo de 1137, en Loire, Rhône-Alpes, Francia, a la edad de 76 años.
